title: State-Space Tyre Health Distillation
summary: A new F1 tyre-degradation paper models pit-stop-relevant wear as a latent state with stint resets and reports better one-step forecasts than an AR(1) benchmark under rolling-origin CV.
rationale: A new F1 tyre-degradation paper models pit-stop-relevant wear as a latent state with stint resets and reports better one-step forecasts than an AR(1) benchmark under rolling-origin CV. That suggests your current hand-built wear proxies may be leaving signal on the table; a lightweight latent-state estimate can be used as an extra feature rather than replacing the pipeline.
implementation_hint: Fit a simple per-driver-per-race latent degradation filter on lap times and stint resets using only past laps, then add 3-6 causal features such as latent_tyre_state, latent_state_slope, residual_vs_expected_laptime, and post-pit reset magnitude to the existing GBDT/CatBoost stack.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination around ambiguous mid-stint and late-stint decisions where raw TyreLife and degradation ratios are too noisy.
risk: Easy to leak future information if the latent state is smoothed bidirectionally instead of filtered causally; keep it strictly forward-only inside each driver-race sequence.
